doc.content: Roundup of hyperscaler custom AI ASIC programs vs NVIDIA GPUs, published Feb 23, 2026. Google TPU v7 Ironwood: 'Anthropic disclosed that it deploys more than one million Ironwood chips for Claude inference workloads.' Per-chip specs: '4,614 teraFLOPS (4.6 PFLOPS) of peak FP8 compute per chip' with '192GB of HBM3e memory providing bandwidth exceeding 7.2 TB/s'; 'Google assembles Ironwood chips into pods of 9,216 chips, interconnected via a proprietary optical mesh fabric.' Microsoft Maia 200: 'more than 10 PFLOPS of FP4 compute' and '216GB of HBM3e, the largest capacity among 2026-class custom accelerators'; power '750W at peak' with 'custom firmware, compiler toolchains, and optimized kernels for OpenAI's GPT-series.' Amazon Trainium 3: '2,520 teraFLOPS (2.52 PFLOPS) of FP8 compute per chip with 144GB of HBM3e memory'; 'AWS assembles Trainium 3 into UltraClusters containing up to one million chips'; AWS claims '50% cost reduction compared to equivalent NVIDIA-based instances for supported workloads.' Meta MTIA roadmap: three generations shipping/sampling in 2026 — MTIA v2 (production), v3 (mid-2026), v4 'Santa Barbara' (late 2026, first HBM4). Spend context: 'Combined hyperscaler capital expenditure reaches $660-690 billion in 2026, with approximately 75% directed specifically at AI infrastructure'; 'Google, Microsoft, Amazon, and Meta each plan $60-80 billion in individual AI capex.' Supply constraint: 'TSMC reported 100% capacity utilization for its 3nm node in H1 2026, with demand running approximately three times higher than available supply.' Corroborating figures from The Register (2026-04-07, https://www.theregister.com/2026/04/07/broadcom_google_chip_deal_anthropic_customer/): Anthropic run-rate revenue '$30 billion—up from approximately $9 billion at the end of 2025'; Anthropic TPU consumption '3.5 gigawatts as part of the multiple gigawatts of next generation TPU-based AI compute capacity'; Broadcom to 'develop and supply custom Tensor Processing Units for Google's future generations' and supply networking components through 2031; Broadcom regulatory filing: 'The consumption of such expanded AI compute capacity by Anthropic is dependent on Anthropic's continued commercial success.' From Data Center Frontier (2025-12-03): Anthropic to access 'up to one million TPUs over the life of the partnership,' bringing 'well over 1 GW of new compute online in 2026 alone'; Project Rainier cluster in Indiana holds 'roughly 500,000 Trainium2 chips,' with Anthropic expected to scale to 'more than 1 million Trainium2 chips on AWS by the end of 2025.'

notes: Introl roundup of hyperscaler custom-ASIC programs vs NVIDIA GPUs (forward-signal story). A deep extraction captures the substitution threat: Anthropic deploying >1M Google Ironwood TPUs for Claude inference, AWS Trainium 3's claimed 50% cost reduction vs NVIDIA instances, $660-690B 2026 hyperscaler capex (~75% to AI infra), and TSMC 3nm at 100% utilization. Reward capturing named ASIC programs as competitive-structure signal and honest hypothesis/observed typing on the secondary-tier claims (no invented precision).
